calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Walther for and with (executor) Miriam Kragness.

The Court stated that this is a petition for final distribution, that the Court has questions regarding unusual expenses such as paying travel expenses for certain people to attend the memorial service, asked how this benefits the estate, that it understands that no objections have been filed and that the heirs a okay with the petition and if it is appropriate to charge the estate for the administrator to attend this hearing. Ms. Kragness responded that the 2 relatives whose expenses were paid were the decedent's sister and sister in law, that they helped with the estate by accessing the decedent's computer and obtaining necessary documents off of it, that she is requesting reimbursement for travel expenses to the hearing as it was a continuation from a June date, that in June when she learned the matter was continued she already had airplane tickets and non-refundable lodging but will not argue about it if it is denied. Mr. Walther stated that bond is coming up for renewal and asked if it could be waived. The Court stated that it cannot waive bond until distribution has been made. Mr. Walther asked if the order for final distribution could be nunc pro tunc to avoid the renewal, that the executor is here and is acting in good faith and that it is a waste of the estates money. The Court explained why bonds are in place and that it cannot make an adjudication that there will not be a problem. The Petition for final distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Continued To Jul-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark A. Walther, for and with Petitioner Miriam Kragness.

The Court asked counsel to address the issues of construction funding and credit card indebtedness. Mr. Walther replied that the contractor has been paid in full for work performed up to the beginning of July, 2015, and that the additional work to be performed by the contractor will exceed funds available by $35,000.00. The contractor has agreed to put a lien on the real property for that amount, to be paid from sales proceeds when the property is sold. He stated that time is of the essence, as the contractor will not proceed with the work unless the contract is signed by a personal representative of the estate under letters issued by the Court, and the contractor has threatened to walk off the job. He stated that only a small sum was due on the credit cards, and that he was not aware of any outstanding hospital or medical bills, or of any other creditors of the estate. Regarding the amount of bond to be set, he requested to be notified as soon as that has been determined by the Court, so that he can begin the process of obtaining a bond in the correct amount. The Court stated that an effort would be made to provide that information to Mr. Walther as soon as the correct amount has been calculated. The matter was then taken under submission by the Court.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Continued To Jul-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
